sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a 5-day history of right knee pain, which started after a skiing accident where he twisted his knee while landing from a jump.
He reports swelling, pain, and difficulty climbing stairs or bending due to soreness.
He denies hearing a pop or feeling anything pop during the incident.
He has not had any similar injuries to this knee before.
Past medical history: The patient had his tonsils out, but no other surgeries are noted in his record.
He has a history of hypertension, which he manages with lisinopril 20 mg daily, and he checks his blood pressure at home, which has been around 125/80 most days.
He does not report any side effects from lisinopril.
Review of systems: The patient denies numbness or tingling in his foot, but reports swelling and pain in his right knee.
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20 mg daily for hypertension
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/77 in the office.
Physical exam: The patient has ecchymosis and edema on his right knee, with an effusion present.

There is pain to palpation of the right medial knee, and the patient has decreased range of motion with a negative varus and valgus test, negative Lachman sign, and palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulse.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain in the right knee.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain, right knee; Hypertension; Past medical history of tonsillectomy.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15 mg once daily to help reduce swelling and pain in the right knee.
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any broken bones.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee.
I continued lisinopril 20 mg daily for hypertension and ordered a lipid panel to check on lipid levels.
I administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: The patient will return for follow-up after the results of the x-ray are available, and we will reassess the need for further treatment.
